content: Um, well, last weekend with those coworkers, when they invited me to talk about the project, I just felt kind of stuck in my own mind. I kept thinking everyone would think I didn’t know what I’m talking about, like I wasn’t capable or interesting enough. So, I’d keep agreeing to things they suggested, even if I didn’t really feel comfortable or interested in them. It’s exhausting, knowing I’m there but also feeling left out or judged. I guess I just wanted to make myself seem approachable, so I smiled a lot and nodded along with whatever they said, even if I didn’t really agree. It’s like I’m trying hard not to come across as weird or like I don’t belong here, but it’s harder than pretending to be acceptable. I’m sorry I’m this person, but I can’t stop feeling like maybe I’m just not cut out for this kind of relationship or that I’m destined to be alone. It’s tough because I want to show I’m willing to try new things, even if it means risking disappointment. Do you think I’m being too cautious or uncomfortable about being around people I don’t know? I’d love for someone to recognize me or say something, but I’m not sure how else to go about it. It’s like I’m not really ready to connect with them, and that makes everything more challenging. I appreciate you playing therapist, though! Please let me know how I might improve my ways of speaking up. I’d really appreciate it if you could just stick to those exact rules from that particular moment. Thanks for being such a good friend to me! 😂 _​️

Yeah, this past week has really been tough for me. When my partner started looking more focused on their career, it just felt like everything was falling apart, like they weren’t really wanting to spend time with me. I kept thinking, “Why doesn’t he seem interested? Why is he always pushing himself so much?” I ended up trying to stop him somehow, which only made me feel even worse. I’m just scared that if I don’t keep him, things will get worse or he’ll decide I’m not enough. It’s like no matter how hard I try, I’m just not good enough for him. I guess I feel like I’m stuck in this cycle where I’m always worrying about whether I’m disappointing him or not. It’s exhausting, but I’ve lost count of how many times I’ve tried things that could maybe make me a better partner. I’d rather just let go than face the worst case scenario where things go wrong and I’m left alone again. It’s frustrating, but I don’t know how else to handle it. My mind just goes round and round about these old memories of wanting to connect, but now they just remind me I’m already far from what I wanted. Just knowing those emotions bubble up makes me feel really sad and worthless. I don’t know what to say instead – I guess I just have to pretend it’s not happening. It’s like I’m waiting for someone to crack and realize I’m failing. So, I just tell them I don’t trust my own intuition and ask for a meeting to see if it’s any better. But honestly, I’m still feeling like I’m already not cut out to be his friend. I’m sorry, but I can’t shake off this feeling. It’s like I’m always having to prove myself. I want to be loved, but I’m never going to receive the approval I desire.

I’ve been thinking a lot about that project all week. I keep telling myself everything will fall apart, and then when it does, I’ll just look back and see how pathetic I must be as a team player. It’s like I’m stuck in a cycle where no matter how much time I spend on it, it just gets worse. I’m scared to start because I’m afraid of showing anyone how bad I feel. I guess I’m just afraid to try and risk making a mistake that everyone will notice. It’s exhausting, and honestly, it makes me want to just skip ahead and get out of all this! I wish I could tell someone about it, but I’m too embarrassed. It’s like I’m stuck in that feeling of saying yes, I can’t handle it.

Yeah, the past week was really tough. I got invited to this small get-together with some old friends, and I was super nervous about it all. I kept thinking everyone was whispering and laughing too much, like they were all kind of scattered around the room. I found myself just smiling too much and occasionally texting to avoid eye contact. By the time it started to feel overwhelming, I just felt pretty lost and sad inside. I ended up leaving early because it all just didn’t seem to be getting any easier. I guess I’m afraid if I don’t stay, I’ll just disappoint them or make them feel left out. It’s like I’m always waiting for someone to screw up or say something stupid. I’m sorry I’ve done this to myself, but I don’t know how to stop. I want to change these feelings, but I’m stuck. So, it feels better to just go along with it. Chances are, if they see how I’m feeling, they might not want to continue those relationships. It’s hard to admit I need help, but I’m scared to risk hurting anyone. Honestly, I appreciate it when therapists push me a bit harder though—I usually just prefer to pretend everything’s okay. What do you think? Just imagine if I had said something wrong or didn’t come across well… That’d be too much to expect from someone I care about. I’d rather not face that kind of disappointment. Can you suggest some questions I could ask to find out what’s really going on with me right now? Or maybe tell me if there’s anything I could use to improve? I want to move forward, but I’m just stuck. It’s like I’m never going to get where I want. Please let me know what to do next. Thanks for inviting me! I appreciate you. ( laughed] But thank you for asking those questions too.

Well, this past week was really tough, right? During that talk about planning next year, I wanted to say something about wanting more independence from my partner, but it just kept happening slower than I wanted. Like, every time I tried to explain how much control I feel, they just decided it wasn’t worth discussing, and I ended up just shutting down or agreeing to whatever they said anyway. It’s like I’m always falling apart again, no matter what I say. I guess I’m convinced that maybe I’m too much of a burden for them, or maybe they’re just not going to let go unless I first prove I’m capable. It’s exhausting, knowing that I’m stuck but also feeling like I’m doomed. So, I just kind of withdrew and didn’t really try to push anything forward. It’s frustrating, and honestly, it makes me want to step away and just forget about this whole thing. I don’t know why I’m always being drawn into this endless cycle of trying to change things or pushing back. Just like that, it leaves me feeling sad and exhausted. It’s hard to stop. Can you think of any other ways I could have said something instead of just stepping away? Or if you want to talk about it later, what’d you usually do after that? I’d love for you to give me some ideas or tell me exactly what I did wrong. That way, I know where to start from here and get back to where we started. I appreciate you playing leader here, but it’s just not enough. I need some real, genuine answers. Thanks for playing leader! :D I’ll see you soon. See you later! Love, XXX.

Yeah, the last family meeting was just like trying to get to a place they didn’t want to go. Like, everyone was talking about the same things, yet I felt invisible when they started discussing my career and how it had to match what they wanted for me. It just made me want to leave and not have to deal with it anymore. Honestly, I just stayed quiet because I didn’t want to make things worse or cause any more conflicts. It’s like no matter what I do, it never fits—or feels right for me. I keep telling myself maybe I’m just stuck in this pattern, but I guess I’m here to learn how to live with it. It’s exhausting, but I don’t have much choice. Sometimes I wonder if I’m just going to fall apart completely. It’s like I can’t stop being convinced that someone else is controlling everything about me. I’d rather not face that face head-on. Can you help me figure out how to get through it?

Yeah, the whole thing really hit me hard. When they talked about my career, it just felt like everything was already planned out, like I couldn’t move beyond a certain point without messing up or being seen as inadequate. I kept thinking, “Why do they even care about what I want to do if they’re going to be disappointed?” After a while, I just stopped trying to say anything because it felt pointless and kind of made me feel even worse. It’s like no matter what I do, it always falls flat or doesn’t go as planned. I keep replaying it in my head for hours, which only makes me feel even more useless. I guess I’m scared that if I try to speak up, I’ll just push everyone further away or prove how wrong I am. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I don’t know where to take it from here. I feel like I’m just waiting for someone to come along and fix everything once and for sure. I wish I could tell them I’m having this trouble, but I’m scared they’d see through me. So, I just stay quiet and hope it won’t happen again. It’s frustrating, but I don’t know how to stop feeling this way. I’ll probably never get past this. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I can’t escape what I don’t like. I appreciate you playing therapist, but I really need some real, authentic answers right now. Please give me some truly honest and true responses. It’s like I’m not cut out for this kind of deep talk. I just don’t trust my own mind to understand me. What am I supposed to say? I don’t know. Just tell you what it is. That way, I don’t have to prove to anyone that I’m wrong. Thank you so much for coming to talk to me. It’s been tough,

Um, well… last week, our partner said something about things not being good enough, and I kind of just froze and agreed right away. I didn’t really say anything back because, honestly, I felt like maybe if I spoke up, it’d just make them hate me forever. I guess I just stayed quiet so I wouldn’t upset them anymore, even if that makes everything worse. It’s like I’m trying not to show them how uncomfortable I feel, but then I worry I’ll just make things worse. I don’t know where this is going, and it’s hard to tell if it’s just another part of me resisting change or if it’s actually happening. Just knowing there’s no cure is already making me feel pretty sad and anxious. I’m sorry I’m this person, but I’ve been struggling a lot lately with stuff that doesn’t seem to matter much. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I want to push through, but the other person keeps pushing back. So, it’s nice when I can just be XXX, but really? I need someone to see through me, to believe in me, or at least let me convince them. But until then, I just keep pretending everything’s fine. It’s exhausting, and I’m scared to ask for help because I’m afraid they’ll see how deep inside me I’m hiding. Can you imagine what that situation would feel like if I told them? I’d be like, “Well, why bother? You’re not going to want to listen.” Or maybe I’ll just end up feeling even worse than I already do. It’s frustrating, but I can’t shake this feeling that I’m just not cut out to deal with these situations. So, I just go along with whatever feels easiest, even though I know it’s not going to work. Thank you so much for having me on this session!

Yeah, the family gathering was... really hard for me. I kept noticing how everyone seemed so connected, even though I wasn’t really there, and it just made me feel like I’m left out of the loop. I ended up going away because I didn’t want to make a scene or seem needy, and staying just felt awkward to me. It’s like I’m an extra in the family, but then they all decide to ignore me instead. That sadness just piles on, making me feel really lost and like maybe I’m not really wanted around here. It’s like I’m always being forgotten somewhere. I don’t know why I’m supposed to be the one to get left behind. I guess it’s just a part of me who feels this way, no matter what I do or say. It’s exhausting to keep pretending things aren’t wrong. I’ll probably just go home and stay quiet again. It’s frustrating, but I don’t have a choice. I guess I wish I could come back and find ways to connect again. But honestly, it’s harder to pull off being left out when I’m feeling this way. I’m sorry I had to say this, but it just feels authentic to me. I don’t know if I’m making it right. I’ll have to go through the same thing next time for sure. It’s hard to stop feeling like I’m always being left out or like I’m not really part of this group. I appreciate you taking the lead on this, but I really need some real answers. It’s great that we’re having this talk, but I just feel like I’m being left out. Can you imagine if I hadn’t figured something out? It’d have been easier to just let it go and forget about it. I love being a part of this family, but sometimes I wonder if it’s worth the effort to try and keep up

I looked at my budget last week and... my head just snaps to the part where there’s no money to cover any of these bills. It’s like I’m constantly showing up to a disaster, and I don’t know how to stop feeling like I’m messing things up. I feel like I’m just going to fall apart, not be able to handle anything next week. I’ve avoided looking at job ads because I’m scared of what others might find out about me or feeling like I’m not capable. Honestly, it just makes me feel worse, overwhelmed. I don’t know why I bother trying to get this back in order when it feels like it’s just going to stay the same. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I want to change but also feel like I’m doomed to fail. My mind just goes round and rot after thinking about it. It’s exhausting, really. I’ll probably just go back to avoiding it entirely. It’s hard to bring myself to look at them again. I guess I’m scared that if I bring attention to something, people will think I’m incompetent or not good enough. It’s frustrating, but I don’t have a clue how to fix this. I’m sorry, but I just can’t seem to shake this feeling that I’m failing. I’m sorry, but I don’t know how to start talking about it. It’s like I’m always waiting for someone to make a joke or give me an excuse. I want to say something, but I’m afraid it’ll come off wrong or be seen as weak. I hate feeling like I’m failing, even though I know it’s just another sign that I’m not cut out for this big challenge. It’s exhausting to pretend I can solve problems, but in reality, they’re just constant reminders of how much I’m still struggling. I’m sorry, but I’m not

I’ve been thinking a lot about that letter from my manager… like, every time I look at it, I get this sinking sensation that I’m going to mess up something important. It’s hard to even start writing out my thoughts because I’m terrified that I’ll say the wrong thing or come across as incompetent. I just keep putting things off, instead of actually spending the time and energy to show up for everyone. It’s like playing pretend to stay one step ahead, but I’m really not ready for that outcome. I guess I’m worried that if I mess up, people will see right through how behind I am. It’s exhausting because I want to grow, but I’m scared to fall short. I wish I could go back to that old pattern where I’m always preparing and doubting myself. It feels like no matter what I try, it’s never quite enough. I’m sorry, but I can’t bring myself to stop worrying about showing up. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I want change, but I just don’t trust myself to reach it. Just being there doesn’t make a difference. So, I keep delaying this application, hoping it won’t happen. But then I remember why I’m doing it—just to prove to myself that I’m capable of succeeding. It’s frustrating because I want to be better, but I’m afraid to face the truth. I’m scared of disappointing people just because I’m not prepared or because I’m sure they already think I’m unworthy. Honestly, I feel like I’m just waiting for an answer. It’s hard to imagine anyone buying that they’d let me down again. I’m sorry, but I don’t really feel any less worthy of trying. It’s like nothing I do makes a difference. I just keep thinking, “We’re never going to fix this.” So, I end up here

Yeah, it was really tough when they gave me that project last week. The moment came and went super fast, and then all these people started asking about it, I just shut down. I kept thinking, “What if I don’t understand enough? What’ll people say, too?” I ended up putting things on hold, even spending half-time working on little ideas just to stay on schedule. Honestly, part of me is blaming myself, like I’m just not cut out for this kind of responsibility. It’s like, no matter what I do, it always feels more daunting than fearing being seen as useless or incompetent. I guess I just feel like I’m never going to get around to actually making a difference here. It’s frustrating because I know I should be able to tackle anything big, but somehow it feels like it’s waiting for me. My mom used to tell me, “Don’t let yourself fall further behind,” and I always felt like I was already behind. So, it’s hard to separate myself from feeling like I’m always going to be struggling. I want to change, but I’m just stuck in this loop where I’m already feeling overwhelmed. It’s like no matter what I do, it doesn’t solve the problem or prove that I’m not good enough. So, I’m sorry I came across as so grumpy, but honestly, I didn’t mean to. I just wanted to express how tough it’s been for you. Please, let me see those words again, please? It feels like I’m never going to step up or make a difference. I’d rather not even talk about it, because knowing I’m failing just makes me want to disappear. Thanks for inviting me to this therapy session, therapist. I’ve been really struggling to feel free to share my thoughts. It’s like I’m always stuck in a place where talking isn’t allowed. But

I imagine I was there, but instead of being surrounded by people, it just felt like I was just sitting there, invisible. When I turned to someone, I stayed quiet most of the time, maybe because I thought they’d notice how awkward or strange I was, or maybe I just felt so out of place. I ended up leaving early because it all just got too much. It’s like no matter what I do, I’m just not enough to吸引 anyone. I want to belong, but I keep wondering if I’ll ever be accepted or loved. It’s like I’m always trying to impress them, but then I feel disappointed once they let me go. Honestly, it’s making me feel really lonely and useless. I’ve tried different ways to talk to people, but nothing seems to fix the part where I’m seen as weird or important enough to disappear. What gives me a bad karma? I guess. So, what do you think? I’d like some insight into why I always seem to end up alone at these events.

Ugh, that hate email made me feel like all my day was falling apart. I really wanted to tell them I couldn’t handle it, but then I just stammered and changed plans. It’s like I’m just too much to handle there. I keep thinking, maybe they’re right, maybe I’m just being selfish being around him. I’ll be okay if he gets used to me more than I expects, you know? I guess I just don’t know how to fix things without making a fool of myself. It’s like I’m always failing at being loved. I’m sorry I did this, but I’m scared to face what I’ll probably end up with. I want to change things, but I’m stuck. Thanks for playing along! I’ll probably go through with it next time to try and stop pretending. But honestly, I feel pretty done already. It’s hard to even start talking about it. I’m just waiting for someone to crack. What do you think?

Ugh, I guess that presentation kind of scared me out of my mind. I kept thinking that just being there would just prove I’m not cut out for this, like, never really felt that way before now. It’s like I’m always one step behind what I’m trying to achieve, and when I try, everything just falls apart. I’ve been putting in so much time reviewing everything because I don’t want anyone to see me failing. Honestly, I feel like I’m already going to let everyone down, and that just makes me even more ashamed. It’s like I’m stuck in this cycle where I keep believing I can’t handle this, and then I get scared to even face the prospect of pushing forward. It’s exhausting, playing pretend to avoid that feeling of seeing what I’m about to do wrong. I’d rather not deal with any of those feelings than just keep pretending everything’s fine. It’s frustrating, really. I’m sorry I did this, but I don’t know how else to approach it. What do you think?

Yeah, it was really hard when they picked the restaurant. I felt completely out of place, like I wasn’t really going to want to go to that other place. I just stayed quiet because I didn’t want them to say something that might make me look stupid or give them reason to not like me anymore. It’s like I’m there, ready for the moment, but then I just freeze and agree anyway. I guess I’m scared that if I said no, they’ll start doubting me or not liking me forever. It’s exhausting trying to keep things smooth, especially after such disappointment. I don’t know what I can do to change how this feels. So, I just try to pretend nothing’s wrong to feel better. It’s like I’ve done this before, and everyone knows it’s not true. I’m sorry, but I can’t bring myself to tell them I want to change my mind. Just... it’s too much to pull off. I hope they won’t notice, but I’m afraid they’ll just dismiss me again. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where I want to change, but it feels like a distant dream. What do you think? I’m really struggling with this right now. Can you give me some advice? Or maybe you could suggest something more interesting next time? That’d be good, you know? I’d appreciate it very much. Thanks! 😆*(-.-):-* (I love laughing, but I’m not sure if that means anything to them.) 🧪 (I’m definitely not looking forward to this either.) 🤔 (And honestly, I just wish I could get away from this situation entirely.) 💃 (It feels like I’m always missing out on whatever happens). 😂 (I bet I’m just setting myself up for failure again). 🙣 (I feel like I’m already failing

I imagine the family gathering was really hard for me too. When my parents started criticizing my career and lifestyle, I felt like I wasn’t even there. I just felt completely invisible, like nothing I do makes them disappointed. It’s like no matter what I do, it’s never enough for them or for me to like where I’m going. I kept quiet because I didn’t want to make things worse, but afterward, I just wanted to leave early so I wouldn’t have to face it. It’s like someone told me I couldn’t handle this kind of pressure unless I do something about it. I don’t know why I always end up feeling so stuck like this. It’s like I’m just not strong enough to stand up for myself when I need to.

Um, well, last week my partner suggested going somewhere different for the holiday, and I was like, “No, no, that doesn’t sound right,” but then I kept thinking, “Well, what if they don’t want it either? What if it’s too much and they just shake their head?” Then, I tried a few ways they could handle it, like suggesting some fun things we could do instead, but it didn’t work. Afterwards, I felt really anxious and kind of useless, like maybe I’m just making everything worse instead of helping them stay with us. It’s hard because I want to be around each other, but I’m scared it’ll just prove how wrong I think. I guess I’m just not prepared to handle these kinds of expectations without responding quickly or saying something silly. So, I end up being super accommodating anyway, even though it makes me feel worse. It’s like I’m always afraid that if I don’t keep things perfect, they’ll decide to leave me permanently. I appreciate you playing therapist, but I feel like I’m just not enough. Thanks for giving me this chance to tell my story. I’ll try to be more honest next time. Thanks for being such a great patient!umentary. I love being a patient - it feels authentic and true to me. Thank you so much for being such a great therapist! 😎👍 laughter! 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥 💥

Yeah, this situation really hit me hard. Every time I get a new project, I just feel like I’m letting everyone down, no matter how small the mistake. I keep thinking, “What’s going on here isn’t I the one who needs help the most?” I don’t want to face anyone or even talk to anyone because I know they’re going to see how incompetent I am. It’s exhausting, and honestly, it makes me want to just skip classes or stay late to try and get through. I guess I just feel like I’m falling behind because I’m not capable enough to fix things myself. It’s like no matter what I do, it’s never good enough. I’m sorry I’ve been struggling with this all this week. I need some advice or maybe we can talk about something more recent instead. Can you just tell me what’s really bothering you? Or maybe let me just tell you how it’s been so far. That’ll probably help me figure out what I’m getting into. I’d really appreciate it if you wouldn’t say “no,” because I’m tired of being told no too often. Thank you!

I don’t even want to think about that dinner anymore. It’s like no matter what I do, it just feels like another failure to me. When it comes to talking about myself, I just fall behind and feel completely worthless. I’m like always told I’m not good enough, and honestly, it makes me feel worse even though it probably doesn’t really affect them. I just wanted to leave early so I could get away from all this negativity. It’s exhausting to keep pretending things work until they don’t. I’m sorry I stuck it out there. I don’t know why I ever let myself get stuck like this. Just knowing it won’t change anything makes me even more sad. I’m scared I’m going to mess everything up again. What’s the point of trying when it always ends badly? I’d love some explanation if someone were asking, but I’m just too uncomfortable saying no right now. Why don’t you just stop coming to these big family meetings? I hate being part of them—like I’m invisible or useless. It’s like no matter how much I try, I’m just not good enough for them. So many times I’ve gone through with those same mistakes, only to feel even more down than before. It’s frustrating, and I’m scared to tell them what’s happening inside me. I guess I just don’t have the strength to face the truth. It’s like I’m always afraid of causing a scene where I’m the one being criticized. I hope you’re able to see that I’m not just a victim here. It’s hard to admit that I need help when I’m already feeling so lost and defeated. But I’m convinced, no matter how hard I try, I’ll never truly belong to this family. Please, if you want peace in my life, can you at least let me alone for a moment? I love you,

Yeah, I got that invite, but... I just froze. I’m really anxious about going, like every little thing could go wrong and everyone would notice how awkward I am. I keep thinking they’re just pining after me or maybe even making fun of me because I’m weird or something. I guess I’m scared that if I do show up, they’ll just turn away or ignore me completely. It’s like no matter what I do, it just doesn’t connect with people the way I’d like. I feel kind of lonely at the end of the night, like I don’t belong anywhere. It’s hard to shake off this feeling of being disappointed and left out again. I’m not sure why I’m such a disappointment to them. I guess I just don’t trust myself to go where they want to go. It’s like I’ve tried so many times to fit in and be accepted, but nothing seems to work perfectly. Just knowing I’m not good enough makes me feel even more lost and embarrassed. I’m sorry, but I can’t escape this feeling altogether. It’s like I’m stuck in a loop where I’m hoping to please them somehow, but instead, I just end up feeling even more out of place. So, I’m curious if you ever had a similar experience? It’s like I’m always waiting for someone to remember my weirdness or make fun of me. I hope I’m right about myself, but honestly, I’m not sure I’m ever going to be ready for that acceptance. I’m glad I won’t be able to change who I am. Can you tell me how you feel when you’re invited to a social event? What do you usually anticipate? Or were you invited to a casual dinner, and did you feel nervous about attending? That would help a lot. And what do you usually feel when you first realize you weren’t invited

Yeah, the dinner was tough for me. When my brother got recognized, I just felt like I wasn’t even there. It’s like everyone thinks I’m the one who excelled more, like I don’t deserve to be part of this group. I kept thinking, “Why do I even try? I’ve gotta figure out why I’m so bad to them.” After it happened, I couldn’t stop thinking about how maybe I really am just a failure compared to them. It’s hard not to feel like I’m just not interesting or important enough. I guess I don’t know how to deal with these feelings when I’m around people who seem to value different things. It’s like I’m trying too hard to fit in, but then I end up feeling worse about myself. I want to change things, but I’m stuck in this loop where being different just makes me look worse. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I feel like I’m losing my place in this relationship. So, it’s frustrating to think about how I’m just going to be left behind. I’m sorry I came across, but it just feels easier to pretend nothing happened. Can you imagine how much better I’d be if I could just let go of that feeling? It’s like I can’t get past the old ideas and see the truth. I appreciate you for asking - I really need that feedback. I’ll try to be more open about it next time. It’s hard to trust that I’m actually ready to change when I’m scared of what others might see. But I’m determined to get through this, okay? It’s like my mind won’t let go of that feeling of inadequacy. I’m sorry I did this, but it’s just hard to break free. Thank you for having me. It’s been tough, but asking made me feel lighter. I’ll give it another chance

Yeah, so last week was kind of tough. When my friends talked me into joining that community group, I just felt like I was being pushed away again.
